---
name: Project Ways of Working
about: Create an issue to establish and document ways of working for a new project or collaboration
title: "[WOW]"
labels: ''
assignees: ''
---

# Ways of Working - **<PROJECT/COLLABORATION NAME>**

## Why this matters
Collaborative projects often run into friction because of disagreement on goals, unstated assumptions about tools, decision-making, and who is responsible for what.
Aligning explicitly on these early avoids duplicated effort, mismatched expectations, and unclear ownership later in the project.
This issue exists to make those decisions explicit and documented, rather than assumed.

## Team
- [ ] Document Purpose and Values of all partners, including where aligned and driving this collaboration
- [ ] Document methods of conflict resolution
- [ ] Decide if there is a route for participants or other interested parties to contribute to organising, and develop appropriate mechanisms or call to facilitate leadership contributions
- [ ] Confirm onboarding process for anyone joining the project after it has started
- [ ] Confirm approach to EDIA (accessibility of meetings/materials, inclusive participation routes)
- [ ] Confirm Code of Conduct to be used (RCM Cooperative or other) and link it below:

## Community feedback and reflective practice
- [ ] Document how feedback and learning about this project will be managed

## Responsibilities (RACI)
Confirm roles and responsibilities using a RACI framework: who is **Responsible** (does the work), **Accountable** (owns the outcome), **Consulted** (gives input), and **Informed** (kept updated) for each key area of this project.
- [ ] Confirm organising committee members
- [ ] RACI table drafted for key project areas
- [ ] RACI table reviewed and agreed by all partners
- [ ] RACI table linked or embedded below:

## Tech stack
- [ ] Document collaboration tech-stack, including file storage, project management, materials preparation, and online meeting tool
- [ ] Confirm access/permissions for all partners across these tools
- [ ] Confirm version control/document naming conventions for shared materials

## Data protection
- [ ] Confirm data protection responsibilities and lawful basis across all partners
- [ ] Confirm what personal or sensitive data (if any) will be collected, stored, or shared, and where
- [ ] Confirm which partner is responsible for data protection compliance for shared data

## Comms

### Internal
- [ ] Confirm the agreed communication channels for organising
- [ ] Confirm the agreed communication channels for partner interactions
- [ ] Document meeting schedule, record of notes (including meeting templates), allocation of actions, expectations for sync/async
- [ ] Document the decision-making process, including transparency and what information can be made public and what needs to remain private

### External
- [ ] Identify which public/community comms channels exist for this project (website, mailing list, social accounts, etc.)
- [ ] Confirm who owns or can post to each external channel
- [ ] Note any partner-specific external channels that also need coordinating
- [ ] Flag that the [Communications Notice issue template] should be used for individual comms activities (reports, posts, newsletters), rather than tracked here

### Publishing outputs
- [ ] Confirm licence preferences for outputs (code, documentation, data)
- [ ] Confirm which outputs will be published to Zenodo
- [ ] Add published outputs to the RCM Cooperative Zenodo community
- [ ] Confirm any partner-specific attribution or co-publishing requirements

## Project sustainability and exit
- [ ] Confirm what happens to the collaboration and its outputs at project end (continues, winds down, hands off)
- [ ] Confirm long-term ownership/maintenance responsibility for any tools, repos, or materials produced
